question.text: Currency derivatives trading was launched in India on which exchange and on what date?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Currency derivatives trading in India was launched on August 29, 2008, on the National Stock Exchange (NSE). The first currency pair traded was USDINR. **The Trap:** BSE and MCX-SX also eventually introduced currency derivatives, but NSE was the first exchange to launch them on August 29, 2008. **Cross-Exam Context:** This date is specifically tested in NISM Series I (Currency Derivatives) and Series IX (Merchant Banking) exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember "August 2008 — NSE — Currency Futures." The available pairs are USDINR, EURINR, GBPINR, JPYINR, and cross-currency pairs EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY.
